title: Chingri Malai Curry
description: Every region has a different definition for comfort food. For Bengal it is Chingri Malai Curry. Soft prawns, fresh tender coconut and a gravy that’s simply full of flavour. This one is from the coast, done the Bengali way.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: ½ cup tender coconut flesh strips + for garnish
ingredients.name: ½ cup tender coconut flesh strips + for garnish

steps: Remove the eyes, tentacles and shells of prawns, devein and transfer into a bowl. Wash thoroughly and drain completely.
Add salt and ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and mix well. Set aside for 10 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan, add marinated prawns and sear on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Add ghee to the same pan. Add bay leaves, onion paste and garlic paste, mix and sauté on medium heat till golden brown.
Add ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and red chilli powder and mix well.
Add ½ cup water, mix well and cook for 2 minutes.
Add ½ cup coconut milk, mix well and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1¾ cups coconut milk, mix and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add green chillies, salt and sugar and mix well. Add ¾ cup water and mix well.
Once the mixture comes to a boil, add prawns, garam masala powder and tender coconut strips and mix well. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with slit green chillies, tender coconut strips and serve hot with steamed rice.
